title: Bifrost Configuration Schema
description: Schema for Bifrost HTTP transport configuration

$schema.description: The schema version. This should be set to "https://www.getbifrost.ai/schema"

read_buffer_size.description: Read buffer size in bytes. This controls the size of the buffer used for reading HTTP headers.

version.description: Controls how empty arrays in allow-list fields (models, allowed_models, key_ids, tools_to_execute) are interpreted. Omit or set to 2 for v1.5.0+ semantics: empty = deny all, ["*"] = allow all. Set to 1 to restore v1.4.x semantics: empty = allow all.

source_of_truth.description: Controls how config.json is reconciled with the config store. "split" preserves existing merge behavior. "config.json" makes present config.json sections authoritative during startup sync; missing sections leave database rows untouched.

encryption_key.description: You can set the value as env.<ENV_VAR_NAME> to use an environment variable. We also read encryption key from BIFROST_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable. Note: once set, the encryption key cannot be changed unless you clean up the database. Accepts any string; a secure 32-byte AES-256 key will be derived using Argon2id KDF. If not provided, data will be saved in plain text. Recommended: use a passphrase of at least 16 bytes for better security

env_label.description: Short label (max 10 characters) displayed in the management UI sidebar to identify the environment (e.g. "staging", "prod"). Overrides the BIFROST_ENV_LABEL environment variable when both are set.

drop_excess_requests.description: Whether to drop excess requests when pool is full

initial_pool_size.description: Initial size of the connection pool

prometheus_labels.description: Labels to use for Prometheus metrics

allowed_origins.description: CORS allowed origins (supports "*" or URI strings)

disable_content_logging.description: Disable logging of sensitive content (inputs, outputs, embeddings, etc.)

allow_per_request_content_storage_overri.description: Allow individual requests to override content storage via the x-bf-disable-content-logging header or context key, and to opt in to raw-byte persistence in logs via x-bf-store-raw-request-response. When false (default), the global disable_content_logging setting is authoritative and per-request storage overrides are ignored. Does not control sending raw bytes back to callers — see allow_per_request_raw_override.

allow_per_request_raw_override.description: Allow individual requests to send raw provider request/response bytes back to the caller via the x-bf-send-back-raw-request and x-bf-send-back-raw-response headers. When false (default), the provider-level send_back_raw_request/response settings are authoritative and per-request overrides are ignored. Does not affect raw-byte persistence in logs — see allow_per_request_content_storage_override.

disable_db_pings_in_health.description: Disable DB pings in health check

dump_errors_in_console_logs.description: Dump full error details to the server console logs. Useful for debugging; may be noisy in production.

log_retention_days.description: Number of days to retain logs

enforce_auth_on_inference.description: Require auth (VK, API key, or user token) on inference endpoints

max_request_body_size_mb.description: Maximum request body size in MB

compat.description: Compat plugin configuration for request type conversion, parameter dropping, and parameter value conversion

convert_text_to_chat.description: Convert text completion requests to chat for models that only support chat

convert_chat_to_responses.description: Convert chat completion requests to responses for models that only support responses

should_drop_params.description: Drop unsupported parameters based on model catalog allowlist

should_convert_params.description: Converts model parameter values that are not supported by the model.

header_filter_config.description: Global header filtering configuration for x-bf-eh-* headers forwarded to LLM providers

allowlist.description: If non-empty, only these headers (from x-bf-eh-* prefix) are allowed to be forwarded

denylist.description: Headers to always block from being forwarded

required_headers.description: Headers that must be present on every request. Requests missing any of these headers are rejected with 400. Case-insensitive matching.

logging_headers.description: Headers to capture in log metadata. Values are extracted from incoming requests and stored in the metadata field of log entries.

whitelisted_routes.description: Routes that bypass auth middleware. Requests to these exact paths skip authentication checks.

hide_deleted_virtual_keys_in_filters.description: When true, deleted virtual keys are omitted from logs filter data.

allow_direct_keys.description: Allow callers to bypass the registered key pool by supplying x-bf-direct-key: true and the provider's raw API key in an Authorization (Bearer), x-api-key, or x-goog-api-key header.

routing_chain_max_depth.description: Maximum depth for routing rule chain evaluation

providers.description: Lite only accepts custom provider configurations

governance.description: Governance configuration for budgets, rate limits, customers, teams, virtual keys, and routing rules

virtual_key_id.description: ID of the virtual key this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with provider_config_id)

provider_config_id.description: ID of the provider config this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with virtual_key_id)

model_config_id.description: ID of the model config this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with the other owner IDs)

customer_id.description: ID of the customer this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with the other owner IDs)

team_id.description: ID of the team this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with virtual_key_id and provider_config_id)

calendar_aligned.description: Deprecated: set calendar_aligned on the owner (team / virtual key / access profile) instead. Kept for backward compatibility with older config.json files; ignored unless a reconciler promotes it to its owner.

token_last_reset.description: Last time token counter was reset

request_last_reset.description: Last time request counter was reset

calendar_aligned.description: Deprecated: set calendar_aligned on the owner (team / virtual key / access profile) instead. Kept for backward compatibility with older config.json files; ignored unless a reconciler promotes it to its owner.

budgets.description: Inline budget configurations for this customer. Each entry must have a unique reset_duration. Mutually exclusive with budget_id.

budget_id.description: Single budget reference (pre-declared in governance.budgets). Deprecated in favour of inline budgets.

calendar_aligned.description: Snap the customer's budget and rate-limit reset windows to clean calendar boundaries (day, week, month, year)

source_id.description: Optional external source identifier (e.g. SCIM group ID) used to map this team to an upstream system. Unique when set.

calendar_aligned.description: Snap the team's budget and rate-limit reset windows to clean calendar boundaries (day, week, month, year)

virtual_key_count.description: Computed count of virtual keys associated with this team

business_units.description: Business unit configurations with optional team assignments

team_ids.description: Team IDs to assign to this business unit

roles.description: RBAC role definitions. Roles are created or updated on startup if the config hash changes; system roles and dashboard-created roles are never deleted.

dac.description: Data Access Control scope: own-data (user's own records), team-data (user's team records), all-data (unrestricted). Defaults to all-data.

access_profile.description: Name of the access profile (defined in access_profiles) to attach as the default for this role. Changing this field triggers a re-sync on next startup. Leave unset to let the dashboard manage the assignment.

permissions.description: List of resource+operation permission pairs to grant to this role

resource.description: Permission resource (e.g. Logs, VirtualKeys, Users, ModelProvider, Metrics, Guardrails, AccessProfiles, Roles, BusinessUnits, AuditLogs, Billing)

operation.description: Permission operation (e.g. View, Create, Update, Delete, Download, RunInference, ManageInference, ViewInference)

value.description: The virtual key value. A literal, or an "env.VAR"/"vault.path" reference resolved to plaintext at load time.

is_active.description: Whether the virtual key is active

calendar_aligned.description: Snap all budget resets to calendar boundaries (day, week, month, year)

team_id.description: Associated team ID (mutually exclusive with customer_id)

customer_id.description: Associated customer ID (mutually exclusive with team_id)

access_profile_id.description: Associated access profile ID for direct access profile assignment (mutually exclusive with team_id and customer_id)

provider_configs.description: Provider configurations for this virtual key (empty means no providers allowed, deny-by-default)

routing_rules.description: Routing rules for dynamic provider/model selection based on CEL expressions

pricing_overrides.description: Scoped pricing overrides applied at runtime by the model catalog

model_configs.description: Per-model rate limit and budget configurations

model_name.description: Model name to apply the configuration to

provider.description: Optional provider name to scope this config

scope.description: Scope where this config applies: "global" (default) or "virtual_key"

scope_id.description: Target entity ID for non-global scopes (e.g. virtual key ID). Required when scope != "global"

calendar_aligned.description: Snap this config's budget resets to calendar boundaries (e.g. a monthly budget resets on the 1st) rather than rolling windows. For virtual_key-scoped configs it inherits the virtual key's setting.

budget_id.description: Deprecated — single budget reference. Use budget_ids instead.

budget_ids.description: List of budget IDs (from governance.budgets) to associate with this model config. Supports multiple budgets (e.g. daily + monthly). Replaces budget_id.

rate_limit_id.description: Rate limit ID to associate with this model

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

password.description: Database password. Use password_command instead when the password must be generated dynamically.

password_command.description: Command executed without a shell to produce the database password on stdout for each new physical connection.

command.description: Executable path or name to run

args.description: Arguments passed directly to the executable

timeout.description: Command timeout as a Go duration string (default: 10s)

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum number of idle connections in the pool (default: 5)

max_open_conns.description: Maximum number of open connections to the database (default: 50)

conn_max_lifetime.description: Maximum lifetime for physical database connections as a Go duration string

vault_store.description: Vault store for external secret management. Fields stored as vault.<path> or vault.<path>#<jsonKey> references are resolved through the configured backend.

prefix.description: Path prefix applied to every secret (default: bifrost)

access_mode.description: read_only resolves existing refs only; read_and_write also auto-stores plaintext fields and deletes owned secrets. Defaults to read_only.

password.description: Database password. Use password_command instead when the password must be generated dynamically.

password_command.description: Command executed without a shell to produce the database password on stdout for each new physical connection.

command.description: Executable path or name to run

args.description: Arguments passed directly to the executable

timeout.description: Command timeout as a Go duration string (default: 10s)

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum number of idle connections in the pool (default: 5)

max_open_conns.description: Maximum number of open connections to the database (default: 50)

matview_refresh_interval.description: How often to refresh dashboard materialized views. Go duration string (e.g. '1m', '5m', '1h'). Default 1m. Raise this when matview refresh CPU cost is material on the database instance. Minimum 5s.

conn_max_lifetime.description: Maximum lifetime for physical database connections as a Go duration string

writer.description: Async logging writer queue and batch tuning. Omitted fields use runtime defaults.

max_batch_size.description: Maximum number of log entries to batch before flushing (default: 1000)

batch_interval.description: Maximum time to wait before flushing a partial batch as a Go duration string (default: 5s)

max_batch_bytes.description: Approximate byte-size ceiling for a batch before flushing (default: 314572800)

write_queue_capacity.description: Buffered queue capacity for pending log writes (default: 10000)

deferred_usage_concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent deferred usage database updates (default: 5)

object_storage.description: Optional object storage for offloading LLM log payloads. When configured, full payloads are stored in S3/GCS while the DB keeps lightweight index data.

bucket.description: Bucket name. Supports env var reference (e.g. env.S3_BUCKET)

prefix.description: Key prefix for stored objects (default: bifrost)

compress.description: Enable gzip compression for stored objects. Default: false

region.description: AWS region. Supports env var reference

endpoint.description: Custom S3-compatible endpoint for MinIO/R2. Supports env var reference

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID. Omit to use default credential chain (instance role, env vars, etc.). Supports env var reference

secret_access_key.description: AWS secret access key. Supports env var reference

session_token.description: AWS session token for STS temporary credentials. Supports env var reference

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for STS AssumeRole. Works with static creds or instance role. Supports env var reference

force_path_style.description: Use path-style URLs for S3 (required for MinIO). Default: false

credentials_json.description: GCP service account credentials JSON or file path. Omit to use Application Default Credentials. Supports env var reference

credentials.description: Deprecated: use credentials_json. Kept for backwards compatibility.

project_id.description: GCP project ID override. Supports env var reference

object_storage_exclude_fields.description: LLM log payload field DB column names that should NOT be offloaded to object storage and must remain in the database (e.g. output_message, input_history, raw_request, raw_response). Unknown names are ignored.

retention_days.description: Days to retain log entries. 0 disables retention-based cleanup.

path.description: Path to the plugin (optional, required for dynamic plugins)

version.description: DB-Backed Only. Version metadata persisted on TablePlugin (default: 1). In DB-backed sync, version metadata is considered for plugin replacement/reload decisions.

placement.description: DB-Backed Only. Whether this plugin runs before, after, or as a built-in. Default: post_builtin. Ignored in config.json.

order.description: DB-Backed Only. Position within placement group. Lower values execute earlier. Default: 0. Ignored in config.json.

config.description: Configuration for the telemetry plugin (Prometheus metrics)

custom_labels.description: Custom labels to add to Prometheus metrics

push_gateway.description: Configuration for pushing metrics to a Prometheus Push Gateway for multi-node cluster deployments

enabled.description: Whether to enable pushing metrics to the Push Gateway

push_gateway_url.description: URL of the Prometheus Push Gateway (e.g., http://pushgateway:9091)

instance_id.description: Instance identifier for grouping metrics. If not set, hostname is used automatically.

push_interval.description: How often to push metrics in seconds

basic_auth.description: Basic authentication credentials for the Push Gateway

disable_content_logging.description: Disable logging of request and response content

allow_per_request_content_storage_overri.description: Allow individual requests to override content storage via the x-bf-disable-content-logging header or context key, and to opt in to raw-byte persistence in logs via x-bf-store-raw-request-response. When false (default), per-request storage overrides are ignored. Does not control sending raw bytes back to callers — see allow_per_request_raw_override.

logging_headers.description: List of headers to capture in log metadata

is_vk_mandatory.description: Whether virtual key (x-bf-vk header) is mandatory for all requests

required_headers.description: List of headers that must be present in requests

config.description: Configuration for the Maxim SDK integration plugin

api_key.description: API key for Maxim SDK authentication

log_repo_id.description: Optional default ID for the Maxim logger instance

request_headers.description: Request header name patterns (exact or wildcard like x-custom-*) to capture and attach as trace tags. Note: * captures all headers including sensitive ones like Authorization.

config.description: Configuration for the semantic cache plugin

provider.description: Provider to use for generating embeddings. Required for semantic search; omit it for direct hash mode with dimension: 1.

embedding_model.description: Model to use for generating embeddings in provider-backed semantic caching. Required when provider is set and not allowed in direct-only mode.

ttl.description: Time-to-live for cached responses (supports duration strings like '5m', '1h' or seconds as number, default: 5min)

threshold.description: Cosine similarity threshold for semantic matching (default: 0.8)

dimension.description: Dimension for vector store embeddings. Use 1 for direct (hash-based) caching without an embedding provider.

default_cache_key.description: Default cache key used when no per-request key is provided. When set, all requests without an explicit x-bf-cache-key header will use this value and be cached automatically.

conversation_history_threshold.description: Skip caching for requests with more than this number of messages in conversation history (default: 3)

cache_by_model.description: Include model in cache key (default: true)

cache_by_provider.description: Include provider in cache key (default: true)

exclude_system_prompt.description: Exclude system prompt in cache key (default: false)

config.description: Configuration for the OpenTelemetry plugin. Supports the legacy single-profile shape or the profiles wrapper for multiple collectors.
Configuration for the Datadog APM and metrics plugin

service_name.description: Name of the service to report to Datadog

ml_app.description: ML application name for LLM Observability grouping (defaults to service_name)

agent_addr.description: Combined host:port address of the Datadog Agent for APM traces (agent mode only, can use env. prefix). Overridden by agent_host when set.

agent_host.description: Host of the Datadog Agent for APM traces, provided separately from the port (agent mode only, can use env. prefix). Takes precedence over agent_addr when set. Useful in Kubernetes where the host is injected via the downward API (status.hostIP).

agent_port.description: Port of the Datadog Agent for APM traces (agent mode only, can use env. prefix). Used only when agent_host is set (defaults to 8126); has no effect when agent_host is unset, in which case agent_addr supplies the port.

dogstatsd_addr.description: Combined host:port address of the DogStatsD server for metrics (agent mode only, can use env. prefix). Overridden by dogstatsd_host when set.

dogstatsd_host.description: Host of the DogStatsD server for metrics, provided separately from the port (agent mode only, can use env. prefix). Takes precedence over dogstatsd_addr when set. Useful in Kubernetes where the host is injected via the downward API (status.hostIP).

dogstatsd_port.description: Port of the DogStatsD server for metrics (agent mode only, can use env. prefix). Used only when dogstatsd_host is set (defaults to 8125); has no effect when dogstatsd_host is unset, in which case dogstatsd_addr supplies the port.

custom_tags.description: Additional tags to add to all traces and metrics (values can use env. prefix)

group_traces_by_session.description: Group requests sharing the same x-bf-session-id header into a single APM trace, with each request as a top-level sibling span. Agent mode only; an inbound W3C traceparent takes precedence (default: false)

disable_content_logging.description: Disable logging of message content to Datadog (default: false)

agentless.description: Use agentless mode to send data directly to Datadog APIs (default: false)

api_key.description: Datadog API key, required for agentless mode (can use env. prefix)

request_headers.description: Request header names to capture as span tags

config.description: Configuration for the BigQuery traces plugin

service_account_key.description: Service account key JSON for authentication. Omit to use Application Default Credentials (ADC). Supports env var syntax: "env.MY_VAR".

create_table_if_not_exists.description: Auto-create the table if it does not exist

flush_interval_seconds.description: Interval between buffer flushes, in seconds

buffer_size.description: Max rows to buffer before flushing

custom_labels.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs stored as JSON in the labels column

request_headers.description: Request-header name patterns (exact or wildcard like "x-custom-*") whose values are stored in the request_headers column

config.description: Configuration for the Kafka traces plugin (Enterprise). Publishes completed traces as JSON messages to a Kafka topic, keyed by trace ID.

topic.description: Kafka topic to publish traces to (required)

sasl_enabled.description: Enable SASL authentication. SASL is only activated when both sasl_enabled is true and sasl is set.

sasl.description: SASL credentials, used when sasl_enabled is true

username.description: SASL username. Supports env var syntax: "env.MY_VAR".

password.description: SASL password. Supports env var syntax: "env.MY_VAR".

ca_cert.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate used to verify the broker's TLS certificate. Omit to use the system CA pool. Supports env var syntax: "env.KAFKA_CA_CERT".

batch_size.description: Maximum number of messages batched per write

flush_interval_ms.description: Maximum time (ms) to wait before flushing a batch

auto_create_topic.description: Create the topic at startup if it does not exist

disable_content_logging.description: Strip input/output message content from traces before publishing

request_headers.description: Request-header name patterns (exact or wildcard like "x-custom-*") whose captured values are embedded in published traces

config.description: Configuration for the Google Cloud Pub/Sub traces plugin (Enterprise). Publishes completed traces as JSON messages to a Pub/Sub topic.

topic_id.description: Pub/Sub topic ID — just the ID, not the full resource name (required)

service_account_key.description: Service account key JSON for authentication. Omit to use Application Default Credentials (ADC). Supports env var syntax: "env.MY_VAR".

auto_create_topic.description: Create the topic at startup if it does not exist

disable_content_logging.description: Strip request/response content from traces before publishing

request_headers.description: Request-header name patterns (exact or wildcard like "x-custom-*") whose captured values are included in published traces

plugin_span_filter.description: Controls which plugin hook spans this observability connector exports. Omit to export all plugin spans. Mode "include" exports only the listed plugins; mode "exclude" exports everything except them. Plugin names match the <name> in the span name "plugin.<name>.<stage>".

otel_profile_config.description: OpenTelemetry export profile. This legacy single-profile shape is still accepted directly as the plugin config.

enabled.description: Whether this profile exports traces and metrics

service_name.description: Service name to be used for tracing

trace_type.description: Type of trace to use for the OTEL collector

protocol.description: Protocol to use for the OTEL collector

metrics_enabled.description: Enable push-based metrics export via OTLP. Recommended for multi-node cluster deployments.

metrics_endpoint.description: OTLP metrics endpoint URL (e.g., http://otel-collector:4318/v1/metrics for HTTP or otel-collector:4317 for gRPC)

request_headers.description: Request header name patterns (exact or wildcard like x-custom-*) to capture and emit as span attributes. Note: * captures all headers including sensitive ones like Authorization.

disable_content_logging.description: When true, message content (input/output messages, embeddings, tool definitions, and tool call arguments/results) is dropped from exported spans. Only metadata such as model, tokens, and latency is sent to the collector.

group_traces_by_session.description: When true, requests sharing the same x-bf-session-id header are grouped into a single OTEL trace, each request's root span appearing as a top-level sibling. An inbound W3C traceparent takes precedence, leaving that request on its own distributed trace.

disable_root_span_content.description: When true, input/output message content is dropped from the root span only; the underlying generation (llm.call) span retains the full content. This removes the duplicate input/output stored at the trace level (e.g. the Langfuse trace Input/Output goes empty) to reduce downstream storage.

headers.description: Custom headers for the collector. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution.

tls_ca_cert.description: Path to TLS CA certificate file

insecure.description: Skip TLS verification (ignored if tls_ca_cert is set)

otel_profiles_config.description: OpenTelemetry plugin configuration with one or more export profiles.

routing_target.description: A single weighted routing target within a rule. All fields except weight are optional; omitting provider or model means use the incoming request value. Weights across all targets in a rule must sum to 1.

provider.description: Target provider name (e.g., 'openai', 'azure'). Omit to use the incoming request provider

model.description: Target model name. Omit to use the incoming request model

provider_key_name.description: Optional provider key name. Resolved to internal key_id at config load time. Requires provider.

weight.description: Probability weight for this target (must be > 0). All weights in a rule must sum to 1

routing_rule.description: Routing rule for dynamic provider/model selection

cel_expression.description: CEL (Common Expression Language) expression for rule evaluation

chain_rule.description: If true, re-evaluates routing chain after this rule matches

targets.description: Weighted routing targets. Weights must sum to 1. Omit provider or model to use the incoming request value.

scope_id.description: Entity ID for non-global scopes (required for non-global scope)

query.description: Visual rule builder state for the management UI (react-querybuilder). Omitted unless exported from the dashboard. Not evaluated at routing runtime (cel_expression is). When present, must include combinator and rules[] so arbitrary metadata cannot be mistaken for builder state.

rules.description: Leaf rules (field, operator, value) and/or nested combinator+rules groups from the query builder.

virtual_key_provider_config.description: Provider configuration for a virtual key

weight.description: Weight for load balancing (null opts out of weighted routing)

allowed_models.description: Allowed models for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all models; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

blacklisted_models.description: Models blocked for this provider config even if matched by allowed_models. Use ["*"] to block all; empty array blocks none.

key_ids.description: Key identifiers allowed for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all keys. Semantics depend on version: v2 (default) empty = deny all; v1 empty = allow all. Values are the key IDs.

complexity_tier_boundaries.description: Score thresholds used to classify complexity_tier values

simple_medium.description: Scores below this threshold are SIMPLE

medium_complex.description: Scores below this threshold and at or above simple_medium are MEDIUM

complex_reasoning.description: Scores below this threshold and at or above medium_complex are COMPLEX

complexity_analyzer_keywords.description: User-editable keyword lists for complexity analysis

complexity_analyzer_config.description: Runtime configuration for complexity_tier CEL routing

auth_config.description: Authentication configuration. Deprecated: Use governance.auth_config instead.

disable_auth_on_inference.description: Deprecated and ignored. Use client_config.enforce_auth_on_inference instead.

pricing_sync_interval.description: Pricing sync interval in seconds. Default is 24 hours. Minimum is 3600 seconds (1 hour).

base_url.description: Base URL for the provider (optional, required for Ollama)

extra_headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

enforce_http2.description: Force HTTP/2 on provider connections (relevant for Bedrock and other net/http-based providers)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Disable TLS certificate verification for provider connections. This bypasses server certificate validation and should be used only as a last resort when a trusted CA chain cannot be configured. Prefer ca_cert_pem for self-signed or private CA deployments.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for provider endpoint connections (e.g. self-signed or internal CA). Supports inline PEM or env.VAR_NAME.

stream_idle_timeout_in_seconds.description: Idle timeout per stream chunk in seconds. If no data is received for this many seconds, the stream is closed. Default: 120.

max_conns_per_host.description: Maximum number of TCP connections per provider host. For HTTP/2 (e.g. Bedrock), each connection supports ~100 concurrent streams. Default: 5000.

beta_header_overrides.description: Override default Anthropic beta header support per provider. Keys are header prefixes (e.g. 'redact-thinking-'), values are true (supported) or false (unsupported). Headers not listed use the built-in defaults.

allow_private_network.description: Allow connections to RFC 1918 private IPs (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x). Enable for providers on a k8s pod network, LAN, or private VPC. Loopback addresses (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1) remain allowed regardless of this setting. Link-local addresses (169.254.x.x) are always blocked.

extra_headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

enforce_http2.description: Force HTTP/2 on provider connections (relevant for Bedrock and other net/http-based providers)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Disable TLS certificate verification for provider connections. This bypasses server certificate validation and should be used only as a last resort when a trusted CA chain cannot be configured. Prefer ca_cert_pem for self-signed or private CA deployments.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for provider endpoint connections (e.g. self-signed or internal CA)

stream_idle_timeout_in_seconds.description: Idle timeout per stream chunk in seconds. If no data is received for this many seconds, the stream is closed. Default: 120.

max_conns_per_host.description: Maximum number of TCP connections per provider host. For HTTP/2 (e.g. Bedrock), each connection supports ~100 concurrent streams. Default: 5000.

beta_header_overrides.description: Override default Anthropic beta header support per provider. Keys are header prefixes (e.g. 'redact-thinking-'), values are true (supported) or false (unsupported). Headers not listed use the built-in defaults.

allow_private_network.description: Allow connections to RFC 1918 private IPs (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x). Enable for providers on a k8s pod network, LAN, or private VPC. Loopback addresses (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1) remain allowed regardless of this setting. Link-local addresses (169.254.x.x) are always blocked.

disable_store.description: Disable OpenAI Responses API conversation storage.

value.description: API key value (can use env. prefix)

models.description: Models this key can access. Use ["*"] to allow all models; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

use_for_batch_api.description: Whether this key can be used for batch API operations (default: false)

oneOf.description: Legacy shape: a bare provider-specific identifier. Equivalent to {"model_id": "<value>"}.

model_id.description: Provider-specific identifier sent on the wire (deployment name, inference profile ID, fine-tuned model ID, etc.).

model_name.description: Canonical model name used for pricing, logging, and family inference.

model_family.description: Underlying model family. Used by provider routing without substring-sniffing the wire model ID.

region.description: Per-alias region override (can use env. prefix).

api_version.description: Azure OpenAI api-version override for this alias.

anthropic_version.description: Azure anthropic-version header override for Claude-on-Azure deployments.

endpoint.description: Per-alias Azure endpoint override (can use env. prefix).

project_id.description: Per-alias Vertex project ID override (can use env. prefix).

project_number.description: Per-alias Vertex project number override (can use env. prefix).

inference_profile_arn.description: Per-alias Bedrock inference profile ARN (can use env. prefix).

use_deployments_endpoint.description: Replicate: use the deployments endpoint instead of the predictions endpoint for this alias.

aliases.description: Model alias mappings: each entry maps a user-facing model name to either a bare provider identifier (legacy string shape) or an AliasConfig object carrying the wire identifier plus optional canonical name, family, and provider-specific overrides.

access_key.description: AWS access key (can use env. prefix)

secret_key.description: AWS secret key (can use env. prefix)

session_token.description: AWS session token (can use env. prefix)

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

external_id.description: External ID for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

session_name.description: Role session name for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

batch_s3_config.description: S3 bucket configuration for Bedrock batch operations

prefix.description: S3 key prefix for batch files

is_default.description: Whether this is the default bucket for batch operations

url.description: VLLM server base URL (can use env. prefix)

model_name.description: Exact model name served on this VLLM instance

use_deployments_endpoint.description: Whether to use the deployments endpoint instead of the models endpoint (default: false)

url.description: Ollama server base URL (can use env. prefix)
SGLang server base URL (can use env. prefix)

client_id.description: Azure client ID for Entra authentication (can use env. prefix)

client_secret.description: Azure client secret for Entra authentication (can use env. prefix)

tenant_id.description: Azure tenant ID for Entra authentication (can use env. prefix)

api_version.description: Deprecated: Bifrost now uses the Azure OpenAI v1 API which does not require an api-version parameter.

project_id.description: Google Cloud project ID (can use env. prefix)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

scheme.description: Weaviate server scheme (http or https) - REQUIRED

host.description: Weaviate server host (host:port). If host is without a port number then the 80 port for insecured and 443 port for secured connections will be used.

secured.description: Secured set it to true if it's a secured connection

headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

class_name.description: Class name for Weaviate vector store

redis_config.description: Redis configuration for vector store (also used for Valkey-compatible endpoints)

addr.description: Redis/Valkey server address (host:port) - REQUIRED (can use env. prefix)

username.description: Username for Redis AUTH (optional, can use env. prefix)

password.description: Password for Redis AUTH (optional, can use env. prefix)

use_tls.description: Use TLS for the Redis/Valkey connection (optional)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for Redis/Valkey connections. Use only when a trusted CA cannot be configured.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for Redis/Valkey TLS connections

cluster_mode.description: Use Redis Cluster mode. Required for cluster configuration endpoints; when enabled, db must be 0.

host.description: Qdrant server host - REQUIRED (can use env. prefix)

api_key.description: API key for authentication (optional, can use env. prefix)

max_recv_msg_size_mb.description: gRPC max receive message size in MB (default: 64). Increase when caching large payloads such as base64-encoded image generation responses.

api_key.description: Pinecone API key - REQUIRED (can use env. prefix)

index_host.description: Index host URL from Pinecone console - REQUIRED (e.g., your-index.svc.environment.pinecone.io)

type.description: Clustering mode. 'mesh' (default) is peer-to-peer via memberlist gossip. 'broker' routes all cluster traffic through a central broker process, for platforms without peer-to-peer connectivity (e.g. Cloud Run).

region.description: Region label for cluster deployment (runtime default: unknown)

failure_threshold.description: Number of failed probes before marking as failed

grpc.description: gRPC transport settings for the cluster counter-sync layer. Replaces the memberlist gossip broadcast path for governance usage counters; memberlist continues to handle cluster membership and other entity sync.

port.description: TCP port the gRPC server listens on

dial_timeout_seconds.description: How long to wait when dialing a peer, in seconds

service_name.description: Service name used for discovery (used by consul, etcd, UDP, and mDNS)

bind_port.description: Port to bind for cluster communication

dial_timeout.description: Timeout for discovery dial operations as a Go duration string (e.g. '5s', '1m')

allowed_address_space.description: CIDR notation for allowed address spaces (e.g., ['10.0.0.0/8', '192.168.0.0/16'])

k8s_label_selector.description: Kubernetes label selector for filtering pods

dns_names.description: DNS names to resolve for node discovery. Supports env.VAR_NAME for environment variable substitution.

consul_address.description: Consul server address for service discovery

mdns_service.description: mDNS service name for local network discovery

broker.description: Broker settings, used when type is 'broker'

address.description: host:port of the broker that nodes dial

tls.description: Whether to dial the broker over TLS

auth_token.description: Optional shared secret sent on connect

listen_port.description: Port the broker process serves on (default: 50051)

scim_config.description: SAML/SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) configuration

authServerType.description: 'org' for Org Authorization Server or 'custom' for Custom Authorization Server. Auto-detected from issuer URL when omitted.

apiToken.description: Okta API token for Admin API access

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'sub')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

entra_config.description: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) JWT authentication configuration

tenantId.description: Azure tenant ID or 'common' for multi-tenant applications

clientSecret.description: Client secret (optional, required for token revocation)

cloud.description: Cloud environment: 'commercial' (default), 'gcc-high' for US Government GCC High, or 'dod' for Department of Defense

appIdUri.description: App ID URI for v1.0 tokens (e.g., api://{clientId})

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'oid')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

serverUrl.description: Base URL of the Keycloak server (e.g., https://keycloak.company.com). Must NOT include the /realms/{realm} suffix.

clientId.description: Keycloak client ID for the confidential client used by Bifrost

clientSecret.description: Keycloak client secret. Supports env. prefix for environment variable references (e.g., 'env.KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET')

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'sub')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

load_balancer_config.description: Load balancer configuration for intelligent request routing

direction_selection_enabled.description: Enable adaptive provider (direction) selection. Defaults to true; omit to leave on.

route_selection_enabled.description: Enable adaptive per-key (route) selection. Defaults to true; omit to leave on.

reroute_failed_directions.description: When a pinned provider's direction is unhealthy, re-route the request to a healthy provider. Defaults to false.

prune_failed_fallbacks.description: Drop unhealthy directions from a request's configured fallbacks. Defaults to false.

tracker_config.description: Configuration for tracking route metrics and performance

bootstrap.description: Bootstrap data for initializing load balancer with historical metrics

guardrails_config.description: Guardrails configuration for content moderation and policy enforcement

description.description: Description of what the rule does

cel_expression.description: CEL (Common Expression Language) expression for rule evaluation

query.description: Visual rule builder state for the management UI (react-querybuilder). Omitted unless exported from the dashboard. Not evaluated at runtime (cel_expression is).

rules.description: Leaf rules and/or nested combinator+rules groups from the query builder.

apply_to.description: When to apply the guardrail (input, output, or both)

sampling_rate.description: Percentage of requests to apply this rule to (0-100)

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for rule execution

max_turns_to_send.description: Number of historical conversation turns to send to the guardrail provider; the latest message is always included on top. 0 sends all turns.

evaluation_mode.description: How the rule's turns are sent to its guardrail providers. 'bundled' (default) concatenates all turns into one guardrail call. 'per_turn' sends each turn in its own call, evaluated in isolation, to avoid context-sensitive classifiers (e.g. Bedrock prompt-attack) combining unrelated turns into a false-positive block; this scans every turn but uses more provider calls.

provider_config_ids.description: IDs of provider configurations to use with this rule

id.description: Unique identifier for the provider config

policy_name.description: Name of the specific policy to use

enabled.description: Whether this provider config is enabled

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for provider execution

access_profiles.description: Enterprise access profile templates for RBAC-driven governance controls

calendar_aligned.description: Deprecated: set calendar_aligned on the parent access profile instead. Kept for backward compatibility with older config.json files; the access-profile reconciler promotes any true value here to the profile's top-level calendar_aligned at load time.

token_max_limit.description: Maximum number of tokens allowed in the reset window

request_max_limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the reset window

calendar_aligned.description: Deprecated: set calendar_aligned on the parent access profile instead. Kept for backward compatibility with older config.json files; the access-profile reconciler promotes any true value here to the profile's top-level calendar_aligned at load time.

is_active.description: Whether this access profile is active

tags.description: Optional list of tags for filtering and grouping profiles

calendar_aligned.description: Snap budget and rate-limit reset windows to clean calendar boundaries (day, week, month, year) for this profile

provider_configs.description: Per-provider restrictions and limits for this profile

all_models_allowed.description: Whether all models under this provider are allowed

allowed_models.description: Allowed model names (ignored when all_models_allowed is true)

key_ids.description: Key IDs allowed for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all keys; empty array or omitted denies all keys. Specific IDs restrict access to those keys only.

audit_logs_config.description: Audit logs configuration for CADF-compliant activity logging

disabled.description: Whether audit logging is disabled (default: false)

hmac_key.description: HMAC secret key for signing audit events (minimum 32 bytes). Can use env. prefix for environment variables (e.g., 'env.AUDIT_HMAC_KEY').

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain audit logs (0 means no retention limit)

large_payload_optimization.description: Large payload streaming optimization configuration. Detects large request/response payloads and streams them through without full materialization.

enabled.description: Master toggle for large payload optimization (default: false)

request_threshold_bytes.description: Request body size in bytes above which streaming kicks in (default: 10MB)

response_threshold_bytes.description: Response body size in bytes above which streaming kicks in (default: 10MB)

prefetch_size_bytes.description: How much of the request to prefetch for metadata extraction in bytes (default: 64KB)

max_payload_bytes.description: Maximum allowed payload size in bytes; reject above this (default: 500MB)

truncated_log_bytes.description: How many bytes to store as truncated preview in logs (default: 1MB)

provider_pricing_override.description: Scoped pricing override applied at runtime by the model catalog

virtual_key_id.description: Virtual key ID (required for virtual_key* scopes)

provider_id.description: Provider name (required for provider* scopes; field key remains provider_id for compatibility)

provider_key_name.description: Provider key name alias. Resolved to internal provider key ID at config load time.

match_type.description: How the pattern is matched against model names

pattern.description: Model name pattern to match (exact name or wildcard prefix ending with *)

request_types.description: Request types this override applies to. At least one value is required.

custom_provider_config.description: Custom provider configuration for extending or customizing provider behavior

is_key_less.description: Whether the custom provider requires a key

request_path_overrides.description: Mapping of request type to custom path overriding the default provider path

allowed_requests.description: Allowed request types for the custom provider

circuit_breaker_config.description: Circuit breaker configuration for automatic failover when a provider endpoint degrades

items.description: A single circuit breaker policy that monitors a primary provider+model and redirects to a fallback when the circuit opens

enabled.description: Whether this policy is active. Disabled policies are ignored by all hooks.

primary_model.description: Model to monitor as it appears in requests (e.g. 'gpt-4-ptu')

primary_key_ids.description: Optional list of key UUIDs to monitor individually. Each key gets its own sub-circuit; the main circuit opens only when all listed keys are exhausted. Leave empty to use a single shared circuit for all keys serving this provider+model.

fallback_provider.description: Provider to route to when the circuit is open

fallback_model.description: Model to request from the fallback provider when the circuit is open

condition.description: Response signals that trigger the circuit to open

operator.description: OR (default): circuit opens when any signal matches. AND: circuit opens only when all signals match simultaneously.

signals.description: List of response signals to evaluate

source.description: What part of the HTTP response to inspect

header_name.description: HTTP response header name to inspect

header_value.description: Trips when the header equals this value (case-insensitive). Mutually exclusive with header_contains.

header_contains.description: Trips when the header value contains this substring (case-insensitive). Mutually exclusive with header_value. If neither is set, trips when the header is present.

default_cooldown.description: How long to keep the circuit open when no header-based cooldown is available. Accepts a Go duration string (e.g. '30s', '5m'). Defaults to 30s.

cooldown_header.description: Response header whose value (in milliseconds) overrides default_cooldown (e.g. 'retry-after-ms'). Falls back to default_cooldown when absent or unparsable.

scim_attribute_role_mappings.description: Ordered list of attribute -> role mappings (first match wins).

attribute.description: JWT claim name (supports dot paths, e.g. 'realm_access.roles')

role.description: Bifrost role to assign on match

scim_attribute_team_mappings.description: Attribute -> team mappings (all matches apply). Use value '*' for pass-through.

value.description: Claim value to match, or '*' for pass-through

team.description: Bifrost team slug to assign. In case of '*' value, leave this empty

value.description: Claim value to match. Wildcard '*' will use the claim as the name of the business unit to assign.

business_unit.description: Bifrost business unit slug to assign. In case of '*' value, leave this empty
